mnemonics: CODE BLOCKS CONTAIN LOGIC: Curly Braces { } Organize & Define Execution. Braces Lock Our Code's Key Statements.
keyPoints: A code block is a group of zero or more statements between balanced curly braces { }.
Code blocks define the scope of variables; variables declared inside a block are local to that block.
Blocks can be used anywhere a single statement is allowed, such as in control flow statements like if, for, while, and methods.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use curly braces { } for multi-line if/else or loop statements, which causes only the first line to be executed as part of the control flow.
Attempting to use a variable outside of the block where it was declared, resulting in a compilation error ('cannot find symbol').
